chrome.hint: You can skip anytime. Full guides live in <1>Docs</1> — bookmark what you need.

steps.title: Main navigation
steps.body: Dashboard, Challenges, Submissions, Friends, Leaderboard, Docs, and Replay are always one click away. Alerts and your profile are on the right.

steps.title: Your stats
steps.body: The sidebar shows level, XP, solved challenges, and category breakdown. Use it as a quick read on your progress.

steps.title: Continue from here
steps.body: Featured and recommended challenges appear in the main area. Pick one to read the briefing and start a timed run when you are ready.

steps.title: Catalog navigation
steps.body: The same top bar works on every page. Docs links to guides on ZIP layout, ELO, and troubleshooting.

steps.title: Filter the list
steps.body: Search by name, filter by difficulty and status, and sort. Your progress summary is at the bottom of the sidebar.

steps.title: Open a challenge
steps.body: Click a card to see the public briefing. After you sign in, Start Challenge opens the full specs and the submit workspace with timer and ZIP upload.

steps.title: Start a run
steps.body: Back returns to the catalog. Start Challenge begins your timed session: you will see full endpoints, tests, and the ZIP submit screen. Limits (cooldown, daily cap) apply to students.

steps.title: Read the briefing
steps.body: Difficulty, category, time limit, and XP reward are shown here. The detailed contract appears only after you start—this page is the overview.

steps.title: Full requirements
steps.body: Endpoints, status codes, performance, and hints unlock inside the submit workspace. If you are new, read Docs → First Steps and Preconfigure Project before starting.

steps.title: Fair play rules
steps.body: Daily attempts (UTC) and cooldown between runs keep rankings fair. Staff accounts skip these limits.

steps.title: Session timer
steps.body: The timer keeps running if you leave the tab. When time is up you cannot submit—plan your implementation before you start.

steps.title: Upload your project
steps.body: Only .zip files. Put pom.xml at the root of the archive, include your Spring (or compatible) API, and avoid secrets. Drag and drop or click the zone to browse.

steps.title: Send to the pipeline
steps.body: Submit Solution uploads your ZIP to the build and test pipeline. Wait until it finishes—closing the tab can fail the run. See Docs → Submitting a Challenge for the full checklist.
